questions.question: If all citrus fruits are a good source of Vitamin C, and tangerines are citrus fruits, then:
choices: all citrus fruits have a lot of potassium
all tangerines are a good source of Vitamin C
tangerines have more Vitamin C than other fruits
questions.explanation: ALL citrus has Vitamin C. Tangerines ARE citrus. So tangerines have Vitamin C.

wrongExplanations.0: Backwards. Tangerines are citrus, not other way around.

questions.question: Sylvia notices her dog Spot picks up his food dish when her alarm goes off. What would BEST test if Spot links the alarm to food?
choices: Give Spot a different food and see if he picks up his dish
Wait until Spot picks up his dish, then set the alarm
Set the alarm at different times of day and see if Spot picks up his dish
Play a different alarm sound each day
questions.explanation: Change WHEN the alarm sounds. If Spot still picks up the dish, the alarm IS the cue.

wrongExplanations.3: Tests sound type, not the alarm itself.

questions.question: If MOST mountains have jagged peaks, and Stowe is a mountain in Vermont, then:
choices: Stowe is the only Vermont mountain with a jagged peak
All things with peaks are mountains
Stowe is probably over 15,000 feet high
Stowe probably has a jagged peak
questions.explanation: MOST = more than half. So PROBABLY.

wrongExplanations.0: Facts don't compare Stowe to other mountains.

questions.question: All rectangles have four sides. All squares are rectangles. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Squares ARE rectangles. Rectangles have 4 sides. So squares have 4 sides.

wrongExplanations.0: Trapezoids have 4 sides but aren't rectangles.

questions.question: Maya found her plants grew faster when she played music. Which experiment BEST tests if music caused the growth?
choices: Play music for all her plants and measure growth
Grow two identical groups — one with music, one without — and compare
Ask other gardeners if they play music
Play different music styles and see which plants look healthiest
questions.explanation: Two groups, one variable changed = real experiment.

wrongExplanations.3: Tests music STYLE, not whether music causes growth.

questions.question: No reptiles have fur. All dogs have fur. Therefore:
choices: All animals with fur are dogs
All animals without fur are reptiles
questions.explanation: Reptiles = no fur. Dogs = fur. So no dog can be a reptile.

wrongExplanations.1: Cats have fur and aren't dogs.
wrongExplanations.2: Reptiles have no fur. Dogs have fur. Can't overlap.
wrongExplanations.3: Frogs have no fur and aren't reptiles.

questions.question: Liam thinks watering his plant causes growth. What would WEAKEN his conclusion?
choices: Other plants in the garden also grew
Plants that weren't watered grew the same amount
questions.explanation: If unwatered plants grew JUST AS MUCH, watering didn't cause growth.

wrongExplanations.0: Sunlight could help too but doesn't disprove watering.
wrongExplanations.1: Other plants don't affect Liam's plant.

questions.question: Some athletes are tall. All basketball players are athletes. Therefore:
choices: Some basketball players might be tall
All tall people are basketball players
questions.explanation: SOME athletes are tall. Basketball players are athletes. SOME might be tall.

wrongExplanations.2: Facts don't say anything about short.

questions.question: All birds have feathers. A penguin is a bird. Therefore:
choices: All animals with feathers are penguins
questions.explanation: All birds = feathers. Penguin IS bird. Penguin has feathers.

questions.question: Tom thinks his sneakers help him run faster. What test BEST proves it?
choices: Time himself running 10 times in the new sneakers
Time himself in new and old sneakers, compare
Ask his friends if he runs faster
Run only in new sneakers for a month
questions.explanation: Comparison new vs. old = real test.

questions.question: No mammals can breathe underwater. A whale is a mammal. Therefore:
choices: All animals breathing underwater are not mammals
Both B and C are correct
questions.explanation: Whales can't breathe underwater (B). Anything that CAN must not be mammal (C).

wrongExplanations.1: True but C is also true.
wrongExplanations.2: True but B is also true.

questions.question: Most students who study at least 30 minutes a night get good grades. Maya studies 45 minutes. Therefore:
choices: Maya gets the best grades in her class
All students who get good grades study 45 minutes
questions.explanation: MOST = probably.

questions.question: Sally claims her plant grows faster on the windowsill. BEST test?
choices: Move the plant to the windowsill and measure for a month
Grow two identical plants — one on windowsill, one in corner — compare
Move plant to windowsill, then back to corner
questions.explanation: Two identical plants, two locations, same time = clean comparison.

wrongExplanations.2: Online facts aren't about HER plant.

questions.question: All squares are rectangles. All rectangles have four sides. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Squares are rectangles. Rectangles have 4 sides. Squares have 4 sides.

wrongExplanations.2: All squares have 4 sides per the chain.

questions.question: If it rains, the picnic is cancelled. The picnic is cancelled. What can you conclude?
choices: The picnic was cancelled because of rain
questions.explanation: Rain causes cancellation, but the picnic could be cancelled for OTHER reasons too. We can only say MIGHT be raining.

wrongExplanations.0: Other things could cancel too (illness, etc.).

questions.question: All dogs bark. Buddy doesn't bark. Therefore:
questions.explanation: All dogs bark. Buddy doesn't. So Buddy can't be a dog.

wrongExplanations.0: Then Buddy would bark, but he doesn't.
wrongExplanations.3: All dogs bark — Buddy must not be a dog.

questions.question: Emma notices the lights flicker every time the dryer starts. What's the BEST test that the dryer causes flickering?
choices: Run the dryer 10 times — see if lights flicker each time
Don't run the dryer — see if lights ever flicker
Try both — run dryer sometimes, don't other times — compare
questions.explanation: Compare both conditions to see if dryer is the cause.

wrongExplanations.0: Doesn't show what happens without the dryer.

questions.question: Some flowers are red. All roses are flowers. Can you conclude all roses are red?
choices: No — only some flowers are red
questions.explanation: SOME flowers are red. Roses are flowers. Roses MIGHT be red, but we don't know if all are.

wrongExplanations.1: True statement but doesn't address roses specifically.

questions.question: Every fish has gills. A goldfish is a fish. Therefore:
questions.explanation: All fish have gills. Goldfish is fish. Goldfish has gills.

wrongExplanations.3: Facts say EVERY fish has gills.

questions.question: Marco claims his lucky pencil helps him do better on math tests. What experiment BEST tests this?
choices: Use the pencil on every test for a year
Use the pencil sometimes and a different pencil other times — compare scores
questions.explanation: Compare with vs. without lucky pencil.

wrongExplanations.3: Different pencil isn't a fair test.

questions.question: All cars need fuel. A bicycle is not a car. What can you conclude about a bicycle?
choices: A bicycle does not need fuel
questions.explanation: Facts only say cars need fuel. They DON'T say only cars need fuel — so we can't tell about bicycles.

wrongExplanations.0: Facts don't say bicycles don't need fuel.
wrongExplanations.2: Could be — but answer with 'Cannot tell' is most precise.

questions.question: Tara wants to know if studying with music helps her test scores. What's the BEST experiment?
choices: Study with music for a year and see grades
Study with music for 5 tests and without music for 5 tests — compare
Listen to music more during the day
questions.explanation: 5 with vs. 5 without = real comparison.

questions.question: All triangles have three sides. A pentagon has five sides. Therefore:
choices: A pentagon is not a triangle
questions.explanation: Triangles have 3 sides. Pentagon has 5. So pentagon is not triangle.

questions.question: If I open the window, the cat runs outside. The cat ran outside. What can you conclude?
choices: The window must have been opened
Someone might have opened the window
The cat is now outside permanently
questions.explanation: Window opening makes cat run out, but cat could run out for OTHER reasons (door, etc.). Might.

questions.question: All boats float. The Titanic was a boat. The Titanic sank. What's most likely true?
choices: The Titanic floated for a while before sinking
The Titanic was never a boat
questions.explanation: All boats float (per the fact). But sinking can happen LATER. The Titanic floated initially, then sank.

wrongExplanations.2: Facts say Titanic was a boat.

questions.question: Some books are interesting. All textbooks are books. What can you conclude?
questions.explanation: SOME books are interesting. Textbooks are books. SOME textbooks might be.

questions.question: Carlos wants to test if exercise helps focus. What's the BEST test?
choices: Exercise every day and see if focus improves
Have one group exercise and another not — compare focus
Ask people who exercise about their focus
questions.explanation: Two groups = control + treatment.

questions.question: All squares are quadrilaterals. All quadrilaterals have four sides. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Squares → quadrilaterals → 4 sides. Squares have 4 sides.

questions.question: If a student studies hard, they pass. Anna passed. What can you conclude?
questions.explanation: Studying causes passing, but Anna could have passed for OTHER reasons (easy test).

wrongExplanations.0: Maybe she passed for another reason.

questions.question: All elephants have trunks. Dumbo has a trunk. Can you conclude Dumbo is an elephant?
choices: Maybe — other animals could have trunks
questions.explanation: All elephants have trunks, but other animals (some apes, etc.) could have trunk-like features. We can't be certain.

wrongExplanations.1: Could be — we just don't know for sure.

questions.question: All triangles have three corners. This shape has three corners. What can you conclude?
choices: The shape might be a triangle
The shape is definitely not a triangle
questions.explanation: All triangles have 3 corners. But other shapes could have 3 corners. So MIGHT be triangle.

questions.question: Mia's plants grow taller every week. She concludes they're growing because she talks to them. What's missing?
choices: She doesn't have control plants she doesn't talk to
questions.explanation: Without control plants (no talking), she can't tell if talking is the cause. The plants might grow anyway.

wrongExplanations.1: Volume doesn't matter for the experiment.

questions.question: All sharks live in water. A great white is a shark. Therefore:
choices: Some sharks don't live in water
questions.explanation: All sharks live in water. Great white is shark. Great white lives in water.

questions.question: If I water the plant, it grows. The plant grew. What can you say?
choices: I must have watered the plant
I might have watered the plant
questions.explanation: Watering grows it, but the plant could grow without watering (rain, etc.). MIGHT.

questions.question: All lions are mammals. Tigers are also mammals. What can you conclude?
choices: Lions and tigers are both mammals
questions.explanation: Both lions and tigers are mammals. That's all we can conclude.

questions.question: Dr. Lin notices her morning coffee correlates with her good mood. What's the BEST experiment?
choices: Drink coffee every morning for a month
Drink coffee some mornings, decaf others — compare moods
Ask others if coffee helps mood
questions.explanation: Comparison with vs. without caffeine = real test.

wrongExplanations.3: More isn't a test of cause.

questions.question: Some apples are sweet. All Honeycrisps are apples. Can you conclude all Honeycrisps are sweet?
choices: No — only some apples are sweet
Maybe — some Honeycrisps could be sweet
questions.explanation: SOME apples are sweet. Honeycrisps are apples. So MAYBE/SOME could be sweet.

questions.question: All birds lay eggs. A platypus is not a bird, but lays eggs. What can you conclude?
questions.explanation: Platypus lays eggs but isn't a bird, so non-birds can lay eggs too.

wrongExplanations.2: Facts say platypus is not a bird.
wrongExplanations.3: All birds lay eggs is the fact.

questions.question: If Sara studies at the library, she gets quiet time. Sara got quiet time. What can you conclude?
choices: She might have studied at the library
She didn't study at the library
questions.explanation: Library gives quiet time, but Sara could get quiet time other ways too.

questions.question: All metals conduct electricity. Wood is not a metal. What can you conclude about wood?
questions.explanation: Facts say all metals conduct, but DON'T say only metals conduct. So we can't conclude about wood.

questions.question: All planets orbit the sun. Mars is a planet. Therefore:
choices: All things that orbit are planets
questions.explanation: All planets orbit. Mars is planet. Mars orbits.

questions.question: Coach Tim wants to test if morning practice improves performance. BEST plan?
choices: Have all players practice in the morning for a month
Half practice in morning, half in afternoon — compare
questions.explanation: Two groups, two times = comparison.

questions.question: All bicycles have two wheels. A unicycle has one wheel. Therefore:
choices: A unicycle is not a bicycle
questions.explanation: Bicycles have 2. Unicycle has 1. Different number = not bicycle.

questions.question: Fact: All squirrels collect nuts in fall. It's currently fall. A squirrel ran past Tom. What's most likely true?
questions.explanation: All squirrels collect nuts in fall. It's fall. So this squirrel is probably collecting.

questions.question: If I study, I learn. If I learn, I remember. If I study, what follows?
questions.explanation: Chain logic: study → learn → remember. So study → remember.

questions.question: All ducks quack. Donald quacks. Can you conclude Donald is a duck?
questions.explanation: All ducks quack, but other animals could quack-like or imitate. Maybe.

wrongExplanations.0: Could be a person, parrot, etc.

questions.question: All winning teams practice hard. The Hawks won. What can you conclude about the Hawks?
questions.explanation: All winners practice hard. Hawks won. So Hawks practiced hard.

questions.question: Most kids who eat breakfast feel energetic at school. Will eats breakfast every day. Will is most likely:
questions.explanation: MOST = probably. Will probably feels energetic.

questions.question: All cats purr. Whiskers doesn't purr. Therefore:
questions.explanation: All cats purr. Whiskers doesn't. So Whiskers can't be a cat.

wrongExplanations.3: We can tell — not a cat.

questions.question: Mike thinks his lucky socks help him win games. He wears them every game and wins every game. What's missing from his proof?
questions.explanation: He's never tested NOT wearing them. Without that comparison, he doesn't know if the socks matter.

questions.question: All squares have equal sides. This shape has equal sides. Can you conclude it's a square?
choices: Maybe — other shapes could have equal sides
questions.explanation: All squares have equal sides, but rhombuses and equilateral triangles also have equal sides. Maybe.

questions.question: All days have 24 hours. Tuesday is a day. Therefore:
questions.explanation: All days = 24 hours. Tuesday is a day. Tuesday = 24 hours.

questions.question: Some doctors are surgeons. All surgeons went to medical school. Conclusion?
choices: Some doctors went to medical school
questions.explanation: Surgeons went to med school. Some doctors are surgeons. So some doctors went to med school.

questions.question: All kittens are cats. All cats have whiskers. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Kittens → cats → whiskers. So kittens have whiskers.

questions.question: Lin notices that on rainy days, her dog is grumpy. Best test of cause?
choices: Wait for rain and watch the dog
Compare dog's mood on rainy days vs. sunny days
questions.explanation: Compare both conditions to see if rain causes grumpiness.

questions.question: All vehicles have wheels. A boat doesn't have wheels. Therefore:
choices: A boat is not a vehicle (per these facts)
questions.explanation: Per the strict facts: all vehicles have wheels, boats don't, so boats can't be vehicles. (In real life, boats ARE vehicles, but the FACTS limit our conclusion.)

questions.question: Sam thinks his red shirt makes him score more goals. He wears it every game. Best test of his theory?
choices: Wear red half the time, blue half — compare goals
questions.explanation: Compare with vs. without red shirt.

questions.question: All books have pages. A magazine has pages. Can you conclude a magazine is a book?
choices: Maybe — pages alone don't make it a book
questions.explanation: Books have pages, but pages alone don't make something a book. Maybe.

questions.question: All triangles have three angles that add to 180°. This shape's angles add to 200°. Therefore:
choices: This shape is not a triangle
questions.explanation: All triangles = 180. This = 200. Not 180, so not triangle.

questions.question: Most people who exercise feel happy. Pat exercises every day. Pat is most likely:
questions.explanation: MOST = probably. Pat probably feels happy.

questions.question: All students take tests. Tom is a student. Therefore:
questions.explanation: All students = tests. Tom is student. Tom takes tests.

questions.question: All birds can fly. Penguins are birds, but can't fly. What's the issue?
choices: The first claim is wrong — not all birds can fly
questions.explanation: The first 'fact' is actually false. Not all birds can fly. Penguins are the counterexample.

questions.question: If Maya wins, she gets a trophy. Maya doesn't have a trophy. What can you conclude?
questions.explanation: Winning gives trophy. No trophy = didn't win.
